AccordionBase.description: Reflected as `allow-multiple`. When set, multiple items may be open at the same time.

Heading level applied to every item header (2–6). Defaults to 3.
Values outside that range are clamped.
Renders the accordion in its quiet (no-border) visual variant.

Disables all items in the accordion. Individual items may also be
disabled independently.

When set, multiple items may be open at the same time. By default only
one item can be open.

AccordionItemBase.description: Whether the accordion item panel is expanded.

Whether the accordion item is disabled. A disabled item keeps its header
in the tab order but blocks toggling.

The size of the item. Inherited from the parent accordion; controls which
chevron icon is displayed. Has no effect when the item is used standalone.

AssetBase.description: The variant of the asset. When not provided, slot content is rendered (e.g., an image).
Accessible label for the asset’s file or folder variant.

AvatarBase.description: URL of the profile image to display.
Text description of the avatar image.
The size of the avatar. Invalid values fall back to the default (500).
Renders a visual outline around the avatar image.
Renders the avatar at reduced opacity, indicating the entity is inactive or unavailable.
Marks the avatar as decorative, hiding it from assistive technology.

BadgeBase.description: The fixed position of the badge.

Whether the badge is outlined.

Can only be used with semantic variants.

ButtonBase.description:
Whether the button is disabled. Removes focusability and prevents
interaction.

Whether the button is in a pending (busy) state. The button remains
focusable but activation is suppressed.

Accessible label forwarded to the internal `<button>` element as
`aria-label`. Required for icon-only buttons, which have no visible text.

Custom accessible label used during the pending state. When omitted,
the pending label is derived from the resolved non-busy accessible name
plus a busy suffix (e.g. "Save, busy").

ButtonGroupBase.description:
The layout direction of the button group.

Note: this property does NOT set `aria-orientation` on the host because
`aria-orientation` is only meaningful for roles that manage arrow-key
navigation (e.g. `toolbar`, `listbox`). Since button-group uses
sequential Tab navigation (not roving tabindex), the attribute would
be invalid.

Whether all buttons in the group are disabled. When `true`, forces
every slotted button child to `disabled`. When `false`, children
retain their individual disabled state — a button slotted with
`disabled` remains disabled even when the group is enabled.
The alignment of buttons within the group along the main axis.

ColorLoupeBase.description:
Whether the loupe is visible. When `false` the loupe is hidden via
CSS opacity and transform transitions.

The CSS color value to display inside the loupe.
Supports any valid CSS color string, including those with alpha
transparency (which reveals the checkerboard behind).

Default is semi-transparent red so the opacity checkerboard is visible
when the component is rendered without a `color` attribute.

DividerBase.description: Whether the divider is vertical. If false, the divider is horizontal. The default is false.
The static color variant to use for the divider.

LinearProgressMixin.description:
Current value. Sanitized via `clampedValue` for rendering and
`aria-valuenow`.

Rare-case accessible-name fallback used when there is no visible
`label` slot content (for example, a data grid of meters).
Renders into `aria-label` on the role element when set.

Custom value text (e.g. `"1 of 4"`). Overrides the auto-formatted
value in both rendered text and `aria-valuetext`.
Position of the label relative to the bar.
Static color override for use on images or colored backgrounds.

MeterBase.description: The variant of the meter. Drives the bar fill color.

ProgressCircleBase.description:
Accessible label for the progress circle.

Used to provide context about what is loading or progressing.
When no accessible name is provided (no label, aria-label, or
aria-labelledby), a default "Loading" label is applied.

Progress value from 0 to 100.

When `null` (indeterminate), the component shows a loading animation.
Setting a number switches to determinate mode. Removing the `progress`
attribute or setting this property to `null` returns to indeterminate.
Values outside 0–100 or non-finite numbers are clamped (non-finite becomes 0).

Reflected to the `progress` attribute when set; the attribute is omitted when indeterminate.

TabBase.description:
Whether this tab is disabled. Disabled tabs are announced as
unavailable by assistive technology and cannot be activated,
but remain focusable via arrow keys within the tablist.

Whether this tab is currently selected. Managed by the parent
`swc-tabs` container — consumers should not set this directly.
Use `swc-tabs[selected]` instead.

Whether the tab is displayed in vertical orientation.

Orientation is primarily driven by the parent `swc-tabs` `direction`
attribute; this property remains for styling and any consumers that read
it explicitly. We can remove it in a future major once vertical layout is
fully parent-driven only—track that cleanup in the component backlog if
it becomes redundant in practice.

Unique identifier for this tab. Used by the parent `swc-tabs`
to match this tab to its corresponding `swc-tab-panel`.

TabPanelBase.description:
Whether this panel is currently visible (its associated tab is
selected). Managed by the parent `swc-tabs` — consumers should
not set this directly.

Unique identifier linking this panel to its corresponding tab.
Must match the `tab-id` attribute on the associated `swc-tab`.

TabsBase.description:
Whether selection follows keyboard focus (`automatic`) or the user
must press Enter or Space to activate (`manual`, default).

The layout direction of the tab list.

**Breaking change:** `'vertical-right'` is no longer supported.
Use `'vertical'` instead.

Whether the entire tab list is disabled. When `true`,
`aria-disabled="true"` is applied to the tablist element and
all interaction is suppressed.

Accessible label for the tablist. Rendered as `aria-label` on the
element with `role="tablist"` in the concrete template.

The `tab-id` of the currently selected tab. Setting this property
updates which tab appears selected and which panel is visible.

TooltipBase.description: The semantic variant of the tooltip.

Preferred placement of the tooltip relative to its trigger. This is always
the consumer's requested side; the resolved physical side (after any
viewport-driven flip) is reflected as `actual-placement`.
The `id` of the trigger element in the same document tree root.

Warm-up delay in milliseconds before the tooltip opens on pointer hover.
Set to `0` to open immediately. Keyboard focus always opens immediately.

When set, the tooltip does not respond to hover or focus events and cannot
be opened. `disabled` takes priority over `open`: setting `open` to `true`
while disabled is a no-op, and disabling an open tooltip closes it. Applies
in `manual` mode as well.

Suppresses automatic hover and focus wiring.
The consumer manages visibility via the `open` property or the popover API.
Pixel gap along the placement axis between the trigger and the tooltip bubble.
Slide along the trigger edge perpendicular to the placement direction, in pixels.
Minimum inset from the viewport edge, in pixels, for collision detection.

Whether the tooltip may reposition to the opposite side when the requested
placement does not fit within the viewport.

When set, the tooltip acts as the trigger's accessible name rather than its description.
Use for icon-only triggers where the tooltip text is the sole accessible name.
